note: Curated sparse waypoints (Phase C, precedence model). A few dated points lift a unit off its derived sector anchor within their date span — a spearhead leading the line, or a correction where the OOB-derived position is plainly wrong. The client interpolates between them and resumes the derived anchor outside the span. [lng, lat]; ascending by date.

de-3rd-ss-panzer-division.note: II SS Panzer Corps jump-off north of Belgorod (Operation Citadel)

su-13th-tank-corps.note: Southern pincer jump-off south of Stalingrad (Operation Uranus)
Driving north-west toward the Kalach link-up
Link-up at Sovetsky / Kalach — the pocket closes

su-26th-tank-corps.note: Northern pincer jump-off from the Kletskaya bridgehead (Operation Uranus)
Seizes the Kalach bridge over the Don
Link-up at Sovetsky — the Stalingrad pocket closes

su-4th-tank-corps.note: Northern pincer breakout with 21st Army (Operation Uranus)
Closing the inner ring west of Stalingrad

su-4th-mechanized-corps.note: Southern pincer breakout with 51st Army (Operation Uranus)
Link-up at Sovetsky with the northern pincer

su-2nd-guards-tank-corps.note: First into Minsk — closes the pocket behind 4th Army

de-2nd-ss-panzer-division.note: Operation Frühlingserwachen jump-off (6th SS Panzer Army, Lake Balaton)
Deepest penetration toward the Danube / Sió canal

de-5th-ss-panzer-division.note: IV SS Panzer Corps attack north of Lake Velence (Balaton)
Pushed back by the Soviet counter-offensive

su-tank-army-1.note: Dug in across the Oboyan road, blunting the German southern pincer (Kursk)
Holds south of Kursk through Prokhorovka

su-army-13.note: Holds the north face at Ponyri/Olkhovatka against Model's pincer (Kursk)
The northern German thrust is stopped
